'''Steve McCurrie''' (born 1 June 1973) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s.

He played representative level rugby league for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]], and at club level for [[Widnes Vikings]] (two spells), [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]], as a {{rlp|pr|hk}} or {{rlp|sr}}, and club level rugby union for [[Bedford RFC]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early life==
Born in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumbria]], McCurrie was educated at [[Whitehaven School]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Sport is just part of John’s DNA |url=https://www.whitehavennews.co.uk/news/17152113.sport-is-just-part-of-johns-dna/ |website=Whitehaven News |access-date=8 November 2024 |date=11 February 2009}}</ref> and played junior [[rugby league]] for [[Hensingham A.R.L.F.C.|Hensingham]]. As a 16-year-old, he played two games for [[Lancaster, Lancashire|Lancaster]]-based [[rugby union]] club [[Vale of Lune RUFC|Vale of Lune]] during the [[1989–90 Courage League National Division Three|1989–90 season]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Youthful promise gives Vale a boost for future |url=https://infoweb.newsbank.com/apps/news/document-view?p=AWGLNB&docref=news/11534A8B8560D8A8 |work=Lancaster Guardian |date=2 November 2006 |via=[[NewsBank]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
McCurrie decided to pursue a career in rugby league, and was signed by [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in 1989.<ref>{{cite news |title=Cooper's sentence is upheld |url=https://www.newspapers.com/image/718014786 |work=The Independent |date=1 December 1989 |page=30 |via=[[Newspapers.com]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> He was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 14-20 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1993 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1992–93 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 1 May 1993.<ref name="1992-1993 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=458|title=1992-1993 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

In 1996, he switched codes to play [[rugby union]] for [[Bedford RFC]], but returned to rugby league two years later and signed for [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: McCurrie back in his old code with Warrington after parting with union |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-mccurrie-back-in-his-old-code-with-warrington-after-parting-with-union-1288334.html |website=The Independent |access-date=8 November 2024 |date=12 December 1997}}</ref>

===International honours===
Steve McCurrie won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] (RL) while at Widnes in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] against Wales ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and Fiji ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and a won cap for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] (RL) while at Widnes in 1993 against France.

==Personal life==
Steve McCurrie is the son of the rugby league footballer; [[Alan McCurrie]].<ref name="Profile at rugby.widnes.tv">{{cite web|url=https://rugby.widnes.tv/playerprofile.php?mid=525|title=Profile at rugby.widnes.tv|publisher=rugby.widnes.tv|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

==Outside of rugby league==
Steve McCurrie now works as an [[Approved Driving Instructor]] in [[Widnes]].
